name: Pad Thai
instructions: Soak rice noodles in warm water for 20 minutes then drain. Heat oil in wok over high heat. Stir-fry tofu until golden, add shrimp and cook 2 minutes. Push to side, scramble egg. Add noodles and sauce (fish sauce, tamarind, sugar). Toss everything together. Add bean sprouts and spring onions. Serve topped with peanuts and lime wedge.

name: Tom Yum Soup
instructions: Bring stock to boil with lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves. Add chillies and mushrooms, simmer 5 minutes. Add shrimp and tomatoes, cook until shrimp is pink. Season with fish sauce and lime juice. Garnish with coriander.

name: Thai Green Curry
instructions: Heat oil in wok, fry curry paste 1 minute. Add half the coconut milk and cook until oil separates. Add chicken and cook through. Pour in remaining coconut milk and add vegetables. Simmer 10 minutes. Season with fish sauce and sugar. Stir in Thai basil.

name: Som Tam (Papaya Salad)
instructions: Shred papaya into thin strips. Pound garlic and chillies in mortar. Add papaya and lightly pound to bruise. Add tomatoes, beans, dried shrimp. Mix in fish sauce, lime juice, sugar. Toss with peanuts and serve immediately.

name: Thai Basil Chicken (Pad Krapow)
instructions: Heat oil over high heat. Fry garlic and chillies 30 seconds. Add minced chicken and stir-fry until cooked. Season with oyster sauce, fish sauce, and soy sauce. Add sugar. Stir in Thai basil until wilted. Serve over rice with a fried egg on top.

name: Massaman Curry
instructions: Brown beef in pot. Add curry paste and fry 1 minute. Pour in coconut milk and bring to boil. Add potatoes, onion, cardamom, cinnamon. Simmer 45 minutes until beef is tender. Season with fish sauce, tamarind, sugar. Top with peanuts.

name: Bibimbap
instructions: Marinate beef with soy sauce, garlic, sesame oil and cook. Blanch spinach and season with sesame oil and garlic. Sauté carrots, zucchini, mushrooms separately. Arrange rice in bowl topped with all vegetables and beef. Top with fried egg. Serve with gochujang.

name: Korean Kimchi Jjigae
instructions: Fry pork belly until lightly browned. Add kimchi and stir-fry 3 minutes. Pour in stock and bring to boil. Add tofu, mushrooms, garlic, gochugaru. Simmer 15 minutes. Season with soy sauce. Drizzle sesame oil and top with spring onions.

name: Bulgogi
instructions: Slice beef thinly. Mix soy sauce, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, grated pear, and sugar for marinade. Marinate beef 30 minutes. Grill or pan-fry beef over high heat with mushrooms until caramelised. Serve topped with sesame seeds and spring onions.

name: Japchae (Korean Glass Noodles)
instructions: Cook glass noodles per packet. Marinate beef with soy sauce, garlic, sesame oil. Stir-fry beef then set aside. Sauté vegetables separately. Combine noodles with all vegetables and beef. Season with soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar. Toss well.

name: Korean Sundubu Jjigae (Soft Tofu Stew)
instructions: Heat oil, fry garlic and gochugaru 1 minute. Add seafood and cook briefly. Pour in anchovy stock and bring to boil. Add soft tofu in large chunks, zucchini, mushrooms. Simmer 5 minutes. Crack egg in and cook to preference. Season with soy sauce.

name: Samgyeopsal (Grilled Pork Belly)
instructions: Grill pork belly slices on a grill pan over medium-high heat until cooked through and slightly crispy, about 4-5 minutes per side. Serve with lettuce leaves for wrapping, alongside garlic, kimchi, and ssamjang dipping sauce.

name: Chicken Adobo
instructions: Marinate chicken in soy sauce and garlic for 30 minutes. Brown chicken in oil. Add vinegar, remaining marinade, water, bay leaves, peppercorns. Bring to boil then simmer 30 minutes until sauce reduces and chicken is tender.

name: Sinigang (Pork Tamarind Soup)
instructions: Boil pork ribs with tomatoes and onion until tender, about 45 minutes. Add tamarind paste and vegetables. Simmer until vegetables are cooked. Season with fish sauce. Serve hot.

name: Pancit Canton
instructions: Stir-fry garlic and onion. Add chicken and shrimp, cook through. Add vegetables and stir-fry 2 minutes. Add stock, soy sauce, oyster sauce. Add noodles and toss until liquid is absorbed. Serve with calamansi.

name: Chicken Tinola
instructions: Sauté ginger, garlic, onion until fragrant. Add chicken and cook until lightly browned. Add fish sauce and stock. Bring to boil and simmer 20 minutes. Add green papaya and cook until tender. Stir in malunggay leaves. Serve immediately.

name: Lumpia Shanghai (Spring Rolls)
instructions: Mix pork with vegetables, garlic, soy sauce, sesame oil, egg. Place filling in wrappers and roll tightly. Deep-fry in hot oil at 180°C until golden brown, about 4 minutes. Drain and serve with sweet chilli sauce.

name: Nasi Goreng
instructions: Fry garlic, shallots, shrimp paste and chillies until fragrant. Add chicken and shrimp, cook through. Push to side and scramble egg. Add cold rice and stir-fry over high heat. Season with kecap manis and soy sauce. Serve topped with fried egg and spring onions.

name: Beef Rendang
instructions: Blend shallots, garlic, chillies, galangal, turmeric, coriander into paste. Fry paste until fragrant. Add beef and coconut milk with lemongrass and kaffir lime leaves. Simmer 2 hours until liquid evaporates and beef is dark and tender. Add toasted coconut.

name: Gado-Gado
instructions: Boil potatoes until tender. Blanch spinach and bean sprouts. Fry tofu and tempeh until golden. Make peanut sauce by cooking peanut butter with coconut milk, kecap manis, lime, garlic, chilli. Arrange all ingredients on plate and drizzle with peanut sauce.

name: Mie Goreng
instructions: Cook noodles until al dente and drain. Stir-fry garlic and shallots. Add chicken and shrimp. Push aside and scramble egg. Add noodles and vegetables. Season with kecap manis, soy sauce, oyster sauce. Toss over high heat. Serve with fried shallots.

name: Chicken Ramen
instructions: Poach chicken breast in stock with soy sauce and mirin, 15 minutes. Slice chicken. Dissolve miso in some hot broth. Cook noodles per packet. Combine broth with miso in bowl. Add noodles, chicken, bamboo shoots, corn. Top with halved egg, nori, spring onions, sesame oil.

name: Teriyaki Salmon
instructions: Mix soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar, ginger for teriyaki glaze. Marinate salmon 15 minutes. Pan-fry salmon 3 minutes each side. Pour glaze into pan and cook 2 minutes, basting salmon. Serve topped with sesame seeds and spring onions.

name: Gyudon (Japanese Beef Bowl)
instructions: Simmer onion in dashi, soy sauce, mirin, sugar until soft. Add thinly sliced beef and cook 3 minutes until done. Serve over steamed rice. Top with soft-boiled egg, pickled ginger, and spring onions.

name: Miso Soup with Tofu
instructions: Heat dashi stock until just below boiling. Add wakame and tofu cubes. Dissolve miso paste in a ladle of broth then add back to pot. Do not boil after adding miso. Serve immediately topped with spring onions.

name: Chicken Gyoza
instructions: Mix chicken with finely chopped cabbage, spring onions, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, sesame oil. Place filling on wrappers and fold. Pan-fry in oil until golden on bottom. Add water, cover and steam 3 minutes until cooked through. Serve with soy-vinegar dipping sauce.

name: Yakitori
instructions: Thread chicken and spring onion pieces onto skewers. Mix soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar for tare sauce. Grill skewers on high heat 3 minutes per side, brushing with tare sauce repeatedly. Serve with remaining tare and sesame seeds.

name: Kung Pao Chicken
instructions: Marinate chicken with soy sauce and cornstarch. Make sauce with soy sauce, vinegar, sugar. Stir-fry dried chillies and peppercorns in hot oil. Add chicken and cook through. Add garlic, ginger, spring onions. Pour in sauce and toss. Add peanuts.

name: Mapo Tofu
instructions: Fry doubanjiang and black beans in oil until fragrant. Add minced beef and cook through. Add garlic, ginger, stock, soy sauce, peppercorns. Add tofu and simmer 5 minutes. Thicken with cornstarch slurry. Top with spring onions and more peppercorns.

name: Chinese Steamed Fish
instructions: Score fish and place on steaming plate with ginger slices. Drizzle with Shaoxing wine. Steam over high heat 8-10 minutes until cooked through. Heat oil until smoking. Pour soy sauce and sesame oil over fish. Top with spring onions and coriander. Pour hot oil over to sizzle.

name: Wonton Soup
instructions: Mix pork, chopped shrimp, ginger, spring onions, soy sauce, sesame oil, white pepper. Place filling in wonton wrappers and fold. Bring stock to boil. Cook wontons in boiling water 3-4 minutes. Transfer to soup bowls. Blanch bok choy in stock. Ladle hot stock over wontons.

name: Egg Fried Rice
instructions: Heat wok until very hot. Add oil, fry garlic briefly. Add cold cooked rice and press to break clumps. Push rice to side, scramble eggs. Mix eggs into rice. Add peas, carrots, spring onions. Season with soy sauce, sesame oil, white pepper. Toss over high heat.

name: Pho Bo (Vietnamese Beef Noodle Soup)
instructions: Char onion and ginger then add to stock pot with beef bones, star anise, cinnamon, cloves. Simmer 3 hours. Strain broth and season with fish sauce. Cook rice noodles. Slice beef thin. Pour boiling broth over noodles and raw beef. Serve with bean sprouts, basil, lime, hoisin, sriracha.

name: Vietnamese Fresh Spring Rolls (Goi Cuon)
instructions: Dip rice paper in warm water until soft. Layer lettuce, herbs, vermicelli, shrimp, pork, cucumber, bean sprouts. Roll up tightly. Make dipping sauce with hoisin, water, and crushed peanuts. Serve immediately.

name: Bun Bo Hue (Spicy Beef Noodle Soup)
instructions: Boil beef shank and pork hock with lemongrass until tender, 1.5 hours. Strain broth and slice meat. Season broth with shrimp paste, fish sauce, chilli oil. Cook vermicelli. Serve noodles in broth topped with sliced meat, spring onions, coriander, bean sprouts.

name: Vietnamese Lemongrass Chicken
instructions: Pound lemongrass, garlic, chillies into paste. Marinate chicken with paste, fish sauce, sugar for 30 minutes. Stir-fry shallots then add chicken. Cook over medium heat until golden and cooked through, about 15 minutes. Top with spring onions.

name: Laksa
instructions: Fry laksa paste in oil until fragrant. Add stock and bring to boil. Pour in coconut milk and simmer 5 minutes. Add shrimp, fish cake, tofu puffs. Cook noodles. Place noodles and bean sprouts in bowl. Ladle hot soup over. Top with egg, coriander, laksa leaves.

name: Nasi Lemak
instructions: Cook rice with coconut milk, pandan leaves, lemongrass until fluffy. Fry anchovies and peanuts until crispy. Fry chicken wings until cooked. Serve rice with sambal, fried anchovies, peanuts, sliced cucumber, halved egg, and chicken.

name: Char Kway Teow
instructions: Heat wok until smoking. Fry garlic and chilli paste. Add shrimp and sausage. Push aside, scramble egg. Add noodles and stir-fry over high heat. Season with dark soy sauce and fish sauce. Add bean sprouts and chives. Toss quickly and serve.

name: Hainanese Chicken Rice
instructions: Poach chicken in stock with ginger and spring onions until just cooked, about 40 minutes. Refresh in ice water. Cook rice in rendered chicken fat with garlic and ginger. Carve chicken and serve over rice with cucumber, spring onions, soy sauce, and chilli sauce.

name: Singapore Chilli Crab
instructions: Stir-fry garlic, ginger, chilli paste in oil. Add crab pieces and stir-fry 5 minutes. Add tomato sauce, soy sauce, sugar, vinegar and some water. Cover and cook 10 minutes until crab is done. Swirl in beaten egg. Garnish with spring onions and coriander. Serve with mantou.

name: Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt and spices for 2 hours. Grill or pan-fry chicken. Sauté onion, garlic, ginger, blend with tomatoes. Fry spices in butter then add tomato mixture. Simmer 15 minutes. Add chicken and cream. Simmer 10 minutes. Add kasuri methi.

name: Dal Tadka
instructions: Boil lentils with turmeric until soft. Mash slightly. Heat ghee and splutter cumin and mustard seeds. Add onion and fry until golden. Add garlic, ginger, tomatoes, spices. Cook until oil separates. Pour over lentils and simmer 5 minutes. Add lemon juice and coriander.

name: Palak Paneer
instructions: Blanch spinach and blend to smooth paste. Fry paneer until golden and set aside. Sauté onion, garlic, ginger in ghee. Add spices and tomatoes and cook until thick. Add spinach puree and simmer 5 minutes. Add paneer and cream. Simmer 3 minutes.

name: Tandoori Chicken
instructions: Score chicken and marinate in yogurt, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, and spices for minimum 4 hours. Grill on high heat or cook in very hot oven (220°C) for 25 minutes, turning once. Serve with lemon wedges, onion rings, and mint chutney.

name: Biryani (Chicken)
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt and spices. Cook onion in ghee until caramelised. Add tomatoes, spices, chicken and cook 15 minutes. Parboil rice. Layer rice and chicken in pot. Top with saffron milk, mint, fried onions. Cook on low heat (dum) for 20 minutes.

name: Chinese Congee (Jook)
instructions: Rinse rice and simmer in large pot of chicken stock for 45-60 minutes until thick and porridge-like, stirring occasionally. Poach chicken separately and shred. Season congee with salt. Serve topped with chicken, sliced century egg, ginger, spring onions, sesame oil.

name: Thai Larb (Minced Meat Salad)
instructions: Cook minced chicken in a dry pan, breaking into pieces. Remove from heat. Mix with fish sauce, lime juice, chilli flakes, toasted rice powder. Add shallots, spring onions, mint, coriander. Toss well and serve with sticky rice and raw vegetables.

name: Korean Doenjang Jjigae (Soybean Paste Stew)
instructions: Bring anchovy stock to boil. Add doenjang paste and stir to dissolve. Add potatoes and onion, cook 5 minutes. Add zucchini, mushrooms, tofu, garlic. Simmer 10 minutes until vegetables are tender. Drizzle with sesame oil. Top with spring onions.

name: Vietnamese Caramelised Ginger Fish (Ca Kho To)
instructions: Make caramel with sugar until amber. Add fish sauce and coconut water. Add fish, ginger, garlic, chillies. Simmer over low heat 20-25 minutes, turning occasionally until sauce reduces and coats fish. Top with spring onions and black pepper.

name: Thai Mango Sticky Rice
instructions: Soak glutinous rice overnight and steam 25 minutes. Heat coconut milk with sugar and salt. Pour over hot rice and mix, let absorb 20 minutes. Slice fresh mango. Serve rice topped with mango slices and drizzle with remaining coconut milk. Sprinkle sesame seeds.

name: Indonesian Sate Ayam (Chicken Satay)
instructions: Blend spices into paste. Marinate chicken with paste and kecap manis for 1 hour. Thread onto skewers. Grill 4-5 minutes per side. Make peanut sauce with peanut butter, coconut milk, chilli, lime, kecap manis. Serve skewers with peanut sauce, cucumber, rice cakes.

name: Japanese Oyakodon (Chicken and Egg Bowl)
instructions: Simmer onion in dashi, mirin, soy sauce, sugar until soft. Add chicken and cook through. Beat eggs lightly and pour over chicken. Cover and cook until just set. Slide over bowl of steamed rice. Top with spring onions and serve with pickles.

name: Tofu Stir-fry with Vegetables
instructions: Press and cube tofu, pan-fry until golden. Remove. Stir-fry garlic and ginger, add vegetables and cook until tender-crisp. Return tofu. Mix soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, cornstarch. Pour over and toss until sauce thickens. Top with spring onions.

name: Filipino Arroz Caldo (Rice Congee)
instructions: Sauté garlic, onion, ginger. Add chicken and cook 5 minutes. Add rice and stir. Pour in stock and simmer 30 minutes, stirring often until thick. Season with fish sauce and safflower for colour. Serve topped with hard-boiled eggs, fried garlic, spring onions, and calamansi.
